free_text: Measurement of cross sections producing short-lived
Department of Energy Engineering and Science
The d-beam energy was 300 keV and the intensity
was 5 milliAmps. A sample transport system of six
pneumatic tubes at angles of 0, 50, 75, 105, 125 and
155 deg to the deuteron beam which covered the neutr.
. The irradiation positions were about 15 cm from
the tritium target. An additional tube set at 0 deg
and at 1.5 cm distance was used when strong neutron
fluxes were required. The sample transfer time was
used for short-lived nuclei and for neutron flux
the effective reaction neutron energy at each
position was determined by the ratio of the
sections. The uncertainty in the neutron energy is
.The samples were sandwiched between two aluminium
.Corrections were made for time fluctuation of neutron
flux, for contribution of low-energy neutrons below
10 MeV, for thickness of samples, for self-absorption
Data are taken from table 2 of 97TRIEST,1,619
Kiyoshi Kawade (2016-01-26): The uncertainty in Table 2
of the Trieste conference proceedings gives the sum of
experimental and nuclear data uncertainties. It is
propagated from the uncertainties in Table 2 of
The square root of the sum of squared of the
experimental error and error in nuclear data
